Research Notes Atlassian Jira AI · Rovo · MCP · DX QPM / SA — April 2026

Jira AI

Personal research notes on Atlassian Intelligence, Rovo (Search, Chat, Agents, Studio), DX, and the Atlassian MCP connector. Includes real screenshots from a live Jira Premium instance.

Source: Rovo AI features in Jira — Atlassian Support

Contents

  1. The three layers: AI / Rovo / DX
  2. Rovo = RAG + Agents + Connectors
  3. Atlassian Intelligence — inline features
  4. Rovo features: Search, Chat, Agents
  5. Agents — anatomy, skills, connectors, MCP
  6. Out-of-box agents
  7. Rovo Studio
  8. DX — engineering intelligence
  9. Atlassian MCP — Claude creates Jira tasks
  10. Activation headache — how to actually turn it on
01

The Three Layers

Layer 1

Atlassian Intelligence

Inline AI engine embedded in Jira/Confluence/JSM. Decompose to subtasks, rewrite descriptions, summarize, NL JQL, JQL fixer. Premium+ only.

Layer 2

Rovo

AI teammate product: Search, Chat, Agents, Studio. Included in all paid plans from April 2025. Previously $20–24/user/month standalone. Standard+.

Layer 3 · acquired $1B

DX

Engineering intelligence. Measures developer productivity & AI ROI — PR cycle time, DORA metrics, qualitative surveys. Part of Software Collection.

02

Atlassian Intelligence — Inline Features

These are the AI features embedded directly in the Jira UI — no chat required. Available on Premium+. They appear inline, contextually, as you work.

📸 "Write with Rovo" — inline AI actions in issue description

Confirmed actions from screenshot:

  • Improve description
  • Summarize writing
  • Improve writing
  • Fix spelling & grammar
  • Brainstorm
  • Make shorter
  • Change tone → sub-options
  • Translate to... → language picker

Also: "Improve Task" button in the issue sidebar (top right panel).

Trigger: click "Write with Rovo" or press Space in empty description field, or /ai slash command.

📸 Summarize work item — BETA
📸 AI work breakdown — suggested subtasks
FeatureStatusPlanPain solved
AI issue summary● BETAPremium+Reading long threads. Shows status, assignee, next steps. "Uses AI. Verify results."
Write / improve description● GAPremium+Blank description field when creating issues. Actions: improve, fix, tone, translate, brainstorm.
AI work breakdown / subtasks● GAPremium+Manually decomposing stories. Shows suggested child issues → "Create all".
AI work breakdown in Jira Plans● GAPremium+Estimating release dates across cross-team plans.
NL JQL (natural language search)● GAPremium+Non-devs unable to write JQL. Type plain English → AI converts to valid JQL.
JQL error fixer● GA Aug 2025Premium+Syntax errors. Shows current query (red) + suggested fix (green) → Accept.
Work creation from Slack / Teams● GAPremium+Threads not becoming Jira tasks. Right-click → Create Jira issue → AI fills fields.
Similar issues surface● GAPremium+Duplicate issues. AI-surfaced similar items on issue creation/view.
AI automation rule generation● GAPremium+Complex automation syntax. Describe in English → AI builds the rule.
Per-project LLM restriction● REMOVED from roadmapWas planned Enterprise Q3 2025Sensitive projects. Was on roadmap, silently removed. Major enterprise blocker.
📸 NL JQL search — "ai research" query
📸 JQL error fixer — suggested query with Accept
03

Rovo = RAG + Agentic Layer + Connectors

Rovo= RAG// retrieval-augmented generation — permission-aware
+ Agentic layer// act / write / create — not just read
+ Permission-aware retrieval// Teamwork Graph = Jira + Confluence + GDrive + Slack + GitHub + 50+
+ Proprietary connectors// 50+ integrations (Synced / Synced Lite / Direct / Smart Link)
+ MCP skills// Early Access: Figma, Amplitude, Canva, GitHub, Intercom...
+ Multiple LLMs// OpenAI (Azure) + Google Gemini (Vertex AI)

Key insight: Rovo is not just an LLM with your data. The LLM call is: system_prompt + RAG_chunks (permission-filtered) + conversation_history + user_message → LLM → response/action. For agents it extends to multi-step ReAct: plan → tool call → result → plan → … → final answer.

04

Rovo Features: Search, Chat, Agents

Rovo Search

Find

Semantic search across Jira + Confluence + Google Drive + Slack + GitHub + 50+ connected apps. Result = link to the source. Respects all existing permissions. 78% accuracy improvement over legacy search (Atlassian claim).

Ask: "Who owns this code?" "What was decided in last sprint retro?" "Find the deployment runbook"

Standard+

Rovo Chat

Ask / Do

Conversational AI — like Claude/ChatGPT/Gemini but with RAG context from your org + ability to take actions (create task, send Slack message, create calendar event). Three modes: standard, Think Deeper, Deep Research (up to 15 min, 30/day).

Docs: Rovo AI Use Cases & Prompt Library

Standard+ + AI ON

Note from research: "On a separate Rovo page it just hangs…." — some pages have loading issues, separate from the activation problem.

05

Agents — Anatomy, Skills, Connectors, MCP

Agent = a configured AI worker with its own identity, knowledge, and abilities. Can be called interactively or triggered automatically.

Scenarios
The core building block. IF user asks X → THEN use Y knowledge + Z skills. Multiple scenarios per agent = context-specific pathways.
System prompt
Behaviour + instructions. Defines role, personality, tone. Always-on regardless of scenario.
Knowledge sources
What the agent can read. Org-wide (default) or scoped: Jira spaces, Confluence, Google Drive. Web search optional. Deep Research mode available per-scenario.
Skills (= Actions)
What the agent can DO. Atlassian-native + third-party. Recommendation: max 5 skills per agent for best performance.
Connectors
How external data gets in. 4 types: Synced, Synced Lite, Direct, Smart Link. See below.
MCP — external
Early Access: connect any MCP server → agent gets new skills. Figma, GitHub, Amplitude, Canva, Intercom, New Relic...

Skills — what an agent can do

Atlassian-native skills

  • Jira: create task, update issue, move to sprint, mark as blocked, search issues
  • Confluence: create page, edit page, publish page, archive
  • JSM: raise a request, triage, respond to ticket

Third-party skills (beta)

  • Slack — send message, read channel data
  • Google Calendar — create event
  • Google Docs — create document
  • Microsoft Teams — send message

Actions appear as if done by the user who called the agent — Slack messages show from your account.

Connector types

Synced
Admin sets up. Full workspace indexed. Best quality for search and chat.
● Best quality
Synced Lite
Per-user connection. Syncs and indexes individual user's content as they connect.
● Good quality
Direct
Live queries via search APIs. No indexing into Atlassian. Results returned in real-time.
● No indexing
Smart Link
No admin setup. Shows results based on individual user's Smart Link permissions and history.
● User-level

How agents are triggered — 4 surfaces

Chat (@agentName)

In Rovo Chat sidebar: @AgentName do X or select from Agents browser. Human-in-the-loop interactive.

Automation rules

Agent as action step. Triggered by schedule, event, or keyword. Fully autonomous — no human needed at runtime.

Inline /rovo or /ai

In Jira/Confluence description or comment fields. Contextual content assistance without leaving the editor.

JSM Portal

Agent as help front-door for end users. Published agent → Surfaces tab → Add to portal. GA January 2026.

06

Out-of-box Agents (20+)

Full list: support.atlassian.com/rovo/docs/atlassian-agents/

Deep dive video: From zero to hero: Building Rovo agents — Team Europe 2025 (Jensen Fleming + Sherif Mansour, Head of AI)

AgentWhat it doesTrigger
Work Item PlannerBreaks large epics into actionable stepsChat: "Break down [epic]"
Issue OrganizerMoves to sprints, assigns epics, bulk updates up to 20 tickets, cleans backlogChat: "Organize my backlog"
Blocker CheckerDetects blocker keywords, changes status to BlockedAutomation rule
Release Notes DrafterGenerates release notes from up to 20 work itemsChat: "Draft release notes for sprint X"
Bug Report WriterWrites complete bug reports with all triage detailsChat: "Write a bug report for..."
Definition of Ready CheckerChecks DoR compliance, suggests what's missingChat or automation
Product Requirements GuideHelps write PRDs with customer empathy + evidenceChat: "Help me write a PRD"
OKR GeneratorCreates/reviews OKRs, finds similar ones in orgChat: "Generate OKRs for team"
Decision DirectorDACI framework review, suggests improvementsChat: "Review my decision doc"
Rovo Ops AgentIncident mgmt: correlates alerts, root cause, timeline, respondersJSM context chat
Service Request HelperHelps agents compose replies from past similar ticketsJSM ticket
Service Triage AgentAuto-triages incoming JSM requestsAutomation rule
Workflow BuilderCreates Jira workflows (transitions, statuses, rules) from plain EnglishChat: "Build a workflow for..."
Project Status MonitorReal-time overview of in-flight projects and prioritiesChat: "Status of all active projects"
Rovo ExpertOnboarding: answers questions about Rovo itselfFirst-time users
07

Rovo Studio — No-code IDE for Non-devs

Status: Open beta April 2026. Admin must opt-in. Access via app switcher. Requires AI enabled. Rovo Chat in Studio acts as a "workflow architect" — you describe a complex org problem, it builds the multi-step solution (which automations, which agents, which apps).

Agents

Full editor with Scenarios, Skills, Knowledge, Surfaces. All created agents in Agents library with analytics (resolution rate, deflection, conversations). Deploy to Chat, Jira, Confluence, JSM Portal, Slack.

Automation rules

Triggers + branches + conditions + actions. Agent as action step. Conditional branching for multi-path workflows. Human-in-the-loop controls. Automation library with usage monitoring.

Apps (Forge runtime)

Production-grade apps from plain English. Runs on Forge — enterprise-grade, permission-controlled Teamwork Graph access. Embedded in Jira, Confluence, Bitbucket, 80+ extension points. Can continue in Forge CLI with code.

Marketplace limitation: As of mid-2025, Marketplace apps (including Forge Remote) cannot programmatically call Rovo agents. The user must go to Chat manually. This means QPM cannot trigger Rovo from within its backend — only automation rules with pre-defined triggers work autonomously.

07b

Rovo Custom Agents on Forge Runtime

Beyond no-code Studio agents, there is a deeper level — agents built with the Forge SDK. This is for developers and Marketplace partners.

Forge rovo:agent module

What it is

A Forge app with a rovo:agent module in manifest.yml. The agent appears in Rovo Chat like any other agent, but all logic lives in your Forge function code. You can connect to any external API, custom databases, or bespoke business logic.

Docs: developer.atlassian.com — rovo:agent module

Forge vs Studio agent

Key difference

Studio agent = configuration (no-code). Forge agent = code. Studio agents only see data within the Teamwork Graph. A Forge agent can call any external service via fetch() — third-party APIs, private data sources, anything.

Constraint: Forge agents only see data from the workspace where the app is installed — no cross-org access.

manifest.yml
modules:
rovo:agent:
- key: risk-agent
name: "My Risk Register Assistant"
prompt: "You are a helpful assistant that manages project risks..."
conversationStarters:
- Fetch my active project risks
actions:
- fetch-all-risks // Forge function — твій код
- create-risk
- update-risk

Actions = Forge functions

Кожен action — це Forge function handler. LLM вирішує який action викликати, передає параметри, отримує результат і вплітає його у відповідь. Точно як MCP tool calls — але всередині Atlassian Forge sandbox.

  • Можна читати/писати в зовнішні API
  • Доступ до Jira/Confluence через Forge APIs
  • Можна публікувати на Atlassian Marketplace
  • Безпека: Forge sandbox, не виходить за межі permissions юзера

How actions work

Each action maps to a Forge function handler. The LLM decides which action to invoke, passes parameters, receives the result, and weaves it into the response — exactly like MCP tool calls, but inside the Atlassian Forge sandbox.

  • Can read/write to external APIs
  • Full access to Jira/Confluence via Forge APIs
  • Can be published to the Atlassian Marketplace
  • Security: Forge sandbox, respects the invoking user's permissions
08

DX — Engineering Intelligence

Acquired Nov 2025 · ~$1B

AI visibility into dev productivity

DX = engineering intelligence platform. Combines qualitative (developer experience surveys, satisfaction) with quantitative (PR cycle time, build failure rates, AI usage, DORA metrics).

Answers: "Is our AI investment paying off? Where are engineers blocked? What's the ROI per developer?"

Added to Software Collection alongside Jira, Bitbucket, Pipelines, Compass, Rovo Dev. Existing customers: Dropbox, Block, Pinterest, Pfizer, Xero.

Status: Acquisition closed Nov 10, 2025. Integration into Software Collection in progress. No standalone Jira UI as of April 2026.

Atlassian + DX: Engineering Intelligence for the AI Era

09

Atlassian MCP — Claude Creates Jira Tasks

Official Atlassian Rovo MCP connector in Claude.ai

Atlassian published an official MCP server available directly in Claude.ai settings (Connectors → Atlassian Rovo). Verified from live Claude.ai session. 7 interactive tools with per-tool permission control (Always allow / Ask / Deny).

Create issue Update issue Retrieve Confluence page + 4 more tools

All actions execute as the authenticated user — Claude acts with your Jira permissions. Actions visible in Jira as created by you.

📸 Claude.ai → Settings → Connectors → Atlassian Rovo (7 interactive tools)

Task created with Claude — live demo

📸 Collection setup screen — Teamwork Collection loading
📸 Task KAN-2 created by Claude via Atlassian Rovo MCP
Rovo → MCP (outgoing)

Rovo Agents + MCP skills

Rovo Agents can connect to external MCP servers to get new skills. Early Access Program. Connect via Admin Hub. Partners: Amplitude, Box, Canva, Figma, GitHub, Intercom, New Relic, Replit. Join EAP: ROVO-309

Claude → Jira (incoming)

Atlassian MCP server in Claude.ai

Claude can create/update Jira issues and read Confluence pages via the Atlassian Rovo MCP connector. Available now in Claude.ai settings. No setup on Atlassian side needed beyond being logged in.

10

Activation Headache — How to Actually Turn It On

Personal notes from research:

"все якось незрозуміло, розписують одне — йдеш і його там немає, падають 404 помилки і взагалі неочевидно все…"

"AI Rovo chat — всюди маркетинг показує а коли купив Standard ніде тої кнопки і немає… Ова, щоб додати треба руками піти в admin.atlassian.com → Apps → і руками додати…"

Documentation not updated, UI changed since Dec 2025, another resource for administration...

Root cause: Two independent switches must both be ON. (1) Rovo activated on your site AND (2) Atlassian Intelligence toggle ON per product. Standard plan gets Rovo (Search, Chat, Agents) but NOT Atlassian Intelligence inline features — that requires Premium+.

1

Confirm eligible plan

Cloud Standard / Premium / Enterprise. Free → no AI. Data Center → no Rovo (cloud connectors only for partial sync).

2

Go to admin.atlassian.com → Rovo → Rovo access

Confirm Jira is NOT in the blocklist. If it is, remove it. This is separate from the AI toggle.

3

Settings → AI-enabled apps → toggle Jira ON

The critical step. Without this, Chat and Agents don't appear even if Rovo is added. Also unlocks inline AI (summaries, NL JQL) if you're on Premium+.

4

Verify business email domain

Org admin must use @yourcompany.com. @gmail.com or @msn.com → Rovo won't activate. Fix: admin.atlassian.com → Directory → Domains → verify.

5

Wait or contact support

Rollout was gradual: Premium/Enterprise April–July 2025, Standard Aug–Oct 2025. Still some sites pending. Contact support with your site URL if steps 1–4 done and Chat still missing.

Where Rovo Chat appears once active: Top navigation bar icon in Jira. Also bottom-right floating button. In issue description: /Rovo or /ai. Browser extension for any tab. admin.atlassian.com — AI (included in Standard+).

Jira AI Research Notes — QPM / SA / AI research — April 8, 2026
